name: Samsung Galaxy Book5 Pro 360
shortDescription: The Samsung Galaxy Book5 Pro 360 is the best 2-in-1 Copilot+ PC — Intel Core Ultra 7 256V, 47 TOPS NPU, 16" 3K AMOLED 120Hz touch, convertible form factor, and Windows 11 Pro for tablet-mode AI use.
useCases: Tablet-mode AI inference with touch and stylus input
Copilot+ AI features in convertible form factor
On-device AI for creative workflows on a 16" AMOLED display
Portable AI workstation that doubles as a presentation device
pros: 16" 3K AMOLED 120Hz touch — best display in the category for content review
Convertible 360° hinge — use as laptop, tent, or tablet for different AI workflows
47 TOPS NPU — full Copilot+ feature support
Windows 11 Pro included — enterprise AI deployment and management
Samsung's premium build quality and display expertise
cons: 16GB RAM — same ceiling as Surface Laptop 7, limits 13B model inference
16" size makes tablet mode heavy for extended handheld use
Fewer available configurations vs Surface Laptop 7 or Zenbook S14
verdict: The Samsung Galaxy Book5 Pro 360 is uniquely positioned as a premium 2-in-1 for AI workflows. The 360° hinge enables tablet mode for reviewing AI-generated content with a stylus, presentation mode for demos, and tent mode for desk use. The 16" 3K AMOLED is arguably the best screen in this roundup. If convertibility and display quality are your priorities over raw RAM, this is the right Copilot+ PC.

faq.question: Can you use the Galaxy Book5 Pro 360 as a tablet for AI?
faq.answer: Yes. In tablet mode (screen folded 360° behind the keyboard), the 16" AMOLED touch display works with Samsung's S Pen. You can run AI apps, review model outputs, annotate AI-generated content, and use Copilot+ touch features. The weight (~1.6kg) makes extended handheld use tiring, but tent mode and stand mode are comfortable.

faq.question: Is Intel Core Ultra 7 256V good for local AI inference?
faq.answer: Yes. The Core Ultra 7 256V is Intel's most efficient laptop chip, built on the 4nm process. It delivers strong single-thread performance for CPU inference and the 47 TOPS NPU handles Copilot+ AI features. For 7B Q4 local models via llama.cpp, expect 4–8 tokens/sec on CPU. OpenVINO NPU acceleration can push this to 10–18 tokens/sec on optimized models.

notFor: Users who won't use tablet/convertible features — non-convertible laptops offer better value
Those needing 32GB RAM — consider ASUS Zenbook S14 instead
entityHeadline: Samsung Galaxy Book5 Pro 360 Review: Best 2-in-1 Copilot+ PC for On-Device AI
